support.summary: No CEO-irreplaceable lever; EB engagement is an RSD/manager task
support.detail: The Economic Buyer (likely Adam Hughes, GM Procurement & Supply Chain) is not yet engaged, but the pack itself assigns that executive briefing to the deal owner's manager once the RFP releases (~15 July), not the CEO. There is no blocked discount, roadmap commitment, presales guarantee, or CEO-to-buyer-CEO peer relationship required at Formal Evaluation stage on a $200k pipeline deal. A VP/CRO can drive EB alignment.
support.metric: $200k amount; EB on zero recorded calls
support.why_not_vp: Executive-buyer alignment and business-case framing is squarely a VP/RSD-manager motion at this pre-RFP stage; nothing here needs the CEO's personal veto or availability.

monitor.reason: Deal is healthy and accelerating (face-to-face booked 07 Jul, RFP tracking to 15 Jul); no monitor trigger has a signal within the last 14 days.
